title: C, C++, and CUDA frontend rendering pipeline
summary: The frontend is a module umbrella combining declaration overlays, raw-line decorations, alignment, highlighting, platform analysis, folding, and linting.
details: view.lua owns full-line declaration overlays and delegates source analysis to parse.lua and chunk construction to render.lua.

aliases.lua owns keyword aliases, function-parameter movement including packed multiline rows, changedtick-keyed C-style function layout facts, function return rewriting, and raw exact CUDA symbol transforms; parse.lua supplies the same exact tables to structured renderers.

parse.lua owns the balanced recursive semantic type grammar and render.lua maps its roles to spelling and color; pointer.lua applies the same grammar to outermost raw templates outside overlay- and flipped-parameter-owned rows.
designated.lua, logic.lua, macro_def.lua, special_members.lua, enum_align.lua, and arrow_align.lua own focused raw-line transforms.
markers.lua owns library-prefix conceal and window-local category highlighting; highlights.lua owns shared highlight definitions.
ppif.lua and fold.lua analyze platform branches and fold structure; scope.lua and overlay_hl.lua provide interaction highlighting; lint.lua reports frontend-specific hazards.

util.lua combines shared source facts, caches, reveal policy, cold-open gating, module state, and decoration lifecycle; its shared skipper owns whole-row exclusions including direct and continued include directives.
aliases.lua owns whole parameter ranges, so its module toggle explicitly refreshes pointer.lua to transfer raw-template ownership without stale or overlapping extmarks.

provenance.note: Umbrella setup enumerates active frontend modules.
Module responsibilities were verified from setup, namespace, refresh, and test call sites rather than legacy prose.

evidence.claim: Static behavior and integrated multi-module output are covered by focused and golden assertions.
Representative modules share the reveal lifecycle contract, and include ownership excludes character-changing renderers across interaction transitions.
Recursive type ownership is verified across aliases on/off cycles, cursor and Visual transitions, source integrity, and a generated real-render corpus.

questions: Which module moves multiline function parameters?
How do parse, render, view, and aliases divide responsibility?
Which shared component prevents aliases from rewriting include paths?

last_verified.method: Static module/namespace audit plus focused interaction coverage of shared include ownership, exact CUDA aliases, recursive semantic types, and dependent module toggles.
